title: Adrenal incidentaloma result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.
examination.rationale: The exact ESE hormone-assessment section supports associated symptom, relevant-negative, patient-concern, measured vital-sign, focused endocrine assessment and clinician-reviewed endocrine laboratory documentation for an incidental adrenal lesion without inferring hormone excess.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

exact_section.heading: Assessment for hormone excess and comorbidity

investigations.text: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.
investigations.rationale: The exact imaging section supports clinician review of previous endocrine correspondence and lesion-imaging context, while the generic field remains conditional and does not classify malignancy risk.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

exact_section.heading: Assessment of malignancy risk by imaging

investigations.text: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.
investigations.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports a clinician-entered plan, completed specialist discussion and arranged follow-up only when documented; it does not recommend surgery, testing or an interval.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.

source.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

sources_used.title: European Society of Endocrinology clinical practice guidelines on the management of adrenal incidentalomas

source_limitations: Lesion size, laterality, imaging phenotype, interval change, specific hormones, comorbidities, interpretation, diagnosis, surgery, treatment and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
